global_cues.us_markets: S&P 500 +0.81% to record 7,259.22; Nasdaq +1.03% to record 25,326.13; Dow +0.73% (+356 pts) to 49,298.25 on May 5. Tech and small-caps led; Russell 2000 also at record. However, post-close oil surge above $110 on US-Iran tensions in Strait of Hormuz capped enthusiasm.
global_cues.european_markets: European indices closed firm on May 5, supported by softer crude earlier in session before late-day Iran-related spike; financials and cyclicals helped sentiment.
global_cues.asian_markets: Japan (Nikkei 225) closed for Golden Week public holiday May 6; last print 59,513 (+0.38%) on May 4. Hang Seng/KOSPI mixed-to-cautious open. GIFT Nifty trading marginally in red, signaling flat-to-slightly-negative open for Indian markets.

fii_dii_summary: FIIs net sellers ₹3,622 Cr on May 5; DIIs net buyers ₹2,603 Cr — DII absorbing ~70% of foreign outflow. YTD FPI outflow already ₹1.92 lakh Cr (exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh Cr); DII inflows ~₹1.7 lakh Cr YTD on sustained SIP support. Persistent FII selling tied to weak INR + Iran risk-off.
earnings_calendar: LT (Larsen & Toubro) - May 6 (Q4 FY26 results post May 5 board meet; key infra/order-book read)
M&M (Mahindra & Mahindra) - May 6 (Q4 FY26 results post May 5 board meet; auto wholesales context)
POLYCAB (Polycab India) - May 6 (Q4 FY26; cables/wires capex theme)
KPITTECH (KPIT Technologies) - May 6 (Q4 FY26; auto-tech ER&D)
BLUESTARCO (Blue Star) - May 6 (Q4 FY26; cooling/AC summer demand)
CGPOWER (CG Power & Industrial Solutions) - May 6 (Q4 FY26)
macro_events: RBI repo rate held at 5.25% (neutral stance) at FY26/27 first MPC; FY27 inflation projected 4.6%, GDP 6.9%
India CPI March 2026 print at 3.4% YoY (12-month high but still below 4% target); food inflation 3.87%
RBI inflation target band reaffirmed at 4% +/- 2% for Apr-2026 to Mar-2031 framework cycle
Heavy Q4 FY26 earnings cluster May 6-9 with 280+ companies reporting; LT and M&M results today are the index movers
sector_themes: Banking under pressure: Nifty Bank -1.18% prior session; HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, SBIN down 0.5-2%; weak rupee + FII selling weighing on financials
IT mixed-to-positive: Nifty IT underperformed since Feb 2026 but deal-win momentum supportive; today's prints from COFORGE, KPITTECH, BSOFT, HEXT key for sentiment
Auto in focus on M&M results and BAJAJ-AUTO Q4 — strong wholesales offset by ~15% sector correction; MARUTI, TATAMOTORS read-through
Pharma defensive bid expected; energy/freight cost pressure a near-term margin headwind; EMCURE, LUPIN, BIOCON, SUNPHARMA, DRREDDY in focus
Capital goods/infra: LT result is the bellwether for order-book momentum; CGPOWER, ABB, THERMAX, POLYCAB cluster reinforces capex theme
Consumer: GODREJCP, DABUR, BLUESTARCO (summer cooling demand), TITAN provide rural + discretionary spend read
Fintech/internet-first: PAYTM, POLICYBZR, SWIGGY, MEESHO results stress-test new-age listed names amid risk-off
risk_flags: USD/INR at fresh record low ~95.46; analysts flagging 96-97 levels; RBI intervention risk elevated
Brent crude $110-114, WTI ~$104; US-Iran exchanged fire in Strait of Hormuz, ceasefire fragile — direct threat to India's import bill (80%+ crude dependency)
Cumulative 2026 FPI outflow >$20 bn (~$19 bn since Iran war start); persistent supply pressure on Indian equities
Rupee weakness + crude spike compounds imported inflation risk; bond yields ticking up; pressure on RBI's neutral stance
Geopolitical: Strait of Hormuz disruption is the dominant tail risk — any tanker incident can spike crude $5-10 intraday
Heavy earnings calendar = stock-specific volatility risk; LT/M&M misses could pressure index disproportionately
brief_summary: Markets set for a flat-to-mildly-negative open: positive US record-close cues (S&P/Nasdaq ATH) offset by Brent above $110 on US-Iran Strait of Hormuz flare-up, USD/INR at record low ~95.46, and persistent FII selling (-₹3,622 Cr) only partially absorbed by DIIs (+₹2,603 Cr). Trading pivots around heavyweight Q4 prints today — LT, M&M, BAJAJ-AUTO, PAYTM, POLYCAB, SHREECEM — with banking weak and IT mid-caps (COFORGE, KPITTECH) under the lens. Bias: defensive, range-bound around Nifty 24,000 with downside risk if crude/INR worsen intraday.
